title: Global Macro x Energy Commodities
notes: Uses EIA and BLS API-keyed endpoints; BLS GET form is preserved from the Claude source catalog.

entries.text: World Bank CPI data is slower-moving than market quotes, but it is still useful in a generalist sweep because it anchors the inflation backdrop in a durable public macro series. A fresh read on the latest annual CPI print helps keep any commodity or rates discussion tied to an openly auditable inflation record instead of to a purely intraday market mood.

The EIA natural-gas futures endpoint gives the sweep a rare public commodity surface that is still machine-readable and operationally usable. A current front-month observation matters because it adds real-economy and energy sensitivity into the session mix instead of leaving macro breadth trapped inside rates, volatility, and crypto-only references.

BLS CPI and EIA natural-gas data are useful together because they connect a broad inflation series with a live commodity-sensitive tape. That combination allows a cleaner analysis post about whether the inflation path and the energy surface are moving in a way that supports calm disinflation, or whether they still point to underlying price pressure that market narratives are downplaying.
